outcome_contract.why: The `prime-outbound-auth-hardening` build removed the compat fallback but left two residual issues: `actorSource: 'reception_proxy'` in audit records is misleading now that the UID is verified (audit clarity issue, not auth correctness), and `PRIME_ACTOR_CLAIMS_SECRET` is absent from local dev env files, causing Reception-side failures (surfaced as 502 by most routes via `inboxApiErrorResponse`, thrown by `buildPrimeActorHeaders()` before Prime is called) with no clear diagnostic indicator in the `.env.example` wording.
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: After this change, `actorSource` in D1 audit records accurately reflects a verified Reception staff origin (`'reception_staff'` or equivalent), existing tests that pin `'reception_proxy'` are updated to the new value, and the existing `PRIME_ACTOR_CLAIMS_SECRET` entries in both `.env.example` files are updated with clearer wording about the Reception-side failure mode (surfaced as 502 by `inboxApiErrorResponse` after `buildPrimeActorHeaders()` throws before Prime is called) so new contributors can diagnose misconfiguration without reading the source.

engineering_coverage.applicable: `actorSource` is labelling only; renaming it does not affect auth correctness; HMAC verification path unchanged
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: No security change; audit correctness improves

engineering_coverage.applicable: `actorSource` in D1 records becomes `'reception_staff'` (accurate); `.env.example` wording improvement reduces time-to-diagnose misconfiguration
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Leaving as `'reception_proxy'` perpetuates misleading audit signal
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Both audit record accuracy and DX improved

engineering_coverage.applicable: 2 test assertions in `review-threads.test.ts` must be updated from `'reception_proxy'` to `'reception_staff'` in `source_metadata_json`; test framework stays the same
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: No test changes if label left alone
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Test update is predictable and low-risk; adds no new test complexity

engineering_coverage.applicable: `actorSource` column is free-form `TEXT`; no schema migration; no breaking contract change
Single PR; no ordering requirement; rollback by reverting string value in 2 source files + 2 test files
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Safe to rollback at any time

open_operator_questions: Should a Prime health-check route be added that calls `validatePrimeActorClaimsConfig()` to surface misconfiguration before the first real request?

stage_payload.decision_summary: The `prime-outbound-auth-hardening` build (TASK-01–07, 2026-03-14) fully removed the `reception_proxy` UID fallback and hardened all 8 Prime mutation endpoints. Three residual issues survive that fix: (1) `actorSource: 'reception_proxy'` is a misleading audit label in two Prime API files (`review-campaign-send.ts:67`, `review-thread-send.ts:66`) now that the UID is verified; (2) `PRIME_ACTOR_CLAIMS_SECRET` is absent from local dev env files (gitignored workstation state) and the existing `.env.example` entries lack enough context to diagnose the first failure mode (a Reception-side failure sur
non_goals: Adding a Prime health-check endpoint (operator input required; out of scope for this plan).
Retroactively rewriting D1 records with the old `actorSource` value.
constraints: `actorSource` is a free-form string column in Prime D1; no schema migration required.
`.env.local` files are gitignored; only `.env.example` files are tracked.
`validatePrimeActorClaimsConfig()` is defined but not wired anywhere today — no startup hook exists on CF Pages Functions; request-time 503 is the only guaranteed signal.

evaluation_criteria.Why it matters: `actorSource` in D1 is free-form audit context (not the identity field — `actorUid` is); misleading values reduce audit-trail readability without affecting auth correctness
`staff-broadcast-send.ts` already uses `'reception_staff_compose'`; new values should be consistent
The change requires updating existing test assertions; scope should be minimal and predictable
`.env.example` updates should reduce time-to-diagnose for misconfigured local setups
Adding a health-check endpoint is operator-gated; analysis should not introduce it unilaterally

options_considered.Upside: Consistent with verified-UID state; matches the semantic of the calling path; reads clearly in audit records
options_considered.Downside: 2 test assertions must be updated (predictable scope)
options_considered.Key risks: None — free-form string, no downstream branching

options_considered.Description: Use the same value already used by `staff-broadcast-send.ts:129`
options_considered.Upside: Maximum consistency — all Reception-originated actions share one label
options_considered.Downside: Loses the distinction between broadcast-send and review-send/campaign-send paths
Perpetuates misleading label after auth hardening; future audit readers will see "proxy" and question whether auth is working

options_considered.Description: Enhance the existing comment block for `PRIME_ACTOR_CLAIMS_SECRET` in both `apps/reception/.env.example` and `apps/prime/.env.example` to explicitly state: "Missing → Reception throws before calling Prime; surfaced as 502 by most routes (not a Prime-side 503)"
options_considered.Upside: Durable (tracked files); zero runtime impact; correct diagnostic starting point for contributors
options_considered.Downside: Does not fix `PRIME_ACTOR_CLAIMS_SECRET` being absent from the actual `.env.local` files (those are gitignored and each dev must set them)

options_considered.Option: B: Add startup preflight call to `validatePrimeActorClaimsConfig` in Prime
options_considered.Description: Wire `validatePrimeActorClaimsConfig` to an existing or new CF Pages Function that runs on first request, logging a clear warning
options_considered.Upside: Faster runtime signal for misconfigured CF deploys
options_considered.Downside: Operator input required (health endpoint decision); CF Pages Functions have no startup hook so this requires a request-time trigger
options_considered.Key risks: Scope expansion beyond this fact-find's remit

options_considered.Downside: Contributes to local-dev friction for new contributors

chosen_approach.Recommendation: Option A for `actorSource` (rename to `'reception_staff'`) + Option A for env-var docs (update `.env.example` wording). These are independent changes that can land in a single PR.
chosen_approach.`actorSource: 'reception_staff_compose'` (Option B) — reduces per-path distinguishability without meaningful benefit; `staff-broadcast-send` already uses this for the broadcast fast-path and conflating review-workflow paths with it loses audit granularity.
chosen_approach.Q: Should a Prime health-check route be added that calls `validatePrimeActorClaimsConfig()` to surface misconfiguration before the first real request?
chosen_approach.Why operator input is required: This requires a decision about whether Prime should expose an unauthenticated health/status endpoint — a product and security trade-off.
chosen_approach.Planning impact: If yes, adds one new CF Pages Function and expands scope. If no (default), plan proceeds with `.env.example` wording only.

rejected_approaches: `actorSource: 'reception_proxy'` (Option C) — perpetuates misleading label; no benefit; rejected.

`actorSource: 'reception_staff_compose'` (Option B) — reduces per-path distinguishability without meaningful benefit; `staff-broadcast-send` already uses this for the broadcast fast-path and conflating review-workflow paths with it loses audit granularity.
Startup preflight/health-check for env var (env Option B) — operator-gated (requires product decision on unauthenticated health endpoint); scope exceeds this plan's residual work; deferred.

validation_implications: TASK-01 and TASK-02 must land in the same PR (TASK-02 is the test update required by TASK-01; they are not independently deployable without a transient CI failure)
TASK-03 is independent and can be included in the same PR or a separate one
CI test run will confirm the 2 updated `review-threads.test.ts` assertions pass
sequencing_constraints: TASK-02 must be done alongside TASK-01; merging TASK-01 without TASK-02 will break CI

areas.seams: Historical records remain stale — acceptable; `review-campaign-send` label fix is preemptive correctness for future direct callers

areas.label: Test assertions for actorSource
areas.future: Test assertions updated to `'reception_staff'` and CI passes

areas.label: Local-dev env-var documentation
areas.current: `.env.example` comment for `PRIME_ACTOR_CLAIMS_SECRET` does not describe the Reception-side failure mode
areas.future: Both `.env.example` files updated with clear comment: "Missing → Reception throws before calling Prime; surfaced as 502 by most routes (not a Prime-side 503)"
areas.steady: All other env var entries and values unchanged
areas.seams: Does not fix the `.env.local` values (gitignored); dev must still set the secret manually
